---
title: "EM 619: Machine Learning for Predictive Analysis"
subtitle: "e-Masters in Data Science for Decision Making (DSDM) · IIT Gandhinagar"
pagetitle: "EM 619: Machine Learning for Predictive Analysis"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Course Information

**Instructors:**

- [Prof. Nipun Batra](https://nipunbatra.github.io) — [nipun.batra@iitgn.ac.in](mailto:nipun.batra@iitgn.ac.in) · Office AB 13/401C · +91 79 2395 2539
- Prof. Ashish Tendulkar — [ashishvt@gmail.com](mailto:ashishvt@gmail.com)

**Teaching Assistants:**

- Abhay Venugopal Pisharodi — [24290001@iitgn.ac.in](mailto:24290001@iitgn.ac.in)
- Veerendra Kumar — [veerendra.kumar@iitgn.ac.in](mailto:veerendra.kumar@iitgn.ac.in)

**Mode:** Online — live sessions over Zoom with recordings

**Session timings:** Thu 8:15–9:45 PM · Sat 11:00 AM–12:30 PM · Sun 10:00–11:30 AM
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
Session timings, dates, and the lecture plan below are based on the previous offering and are **tentative**. Confirmed details for the upcoming term will be announced on the course channel.
:::

## Course Objectives

This course enables students to comprehend fundamental machine learning concepts and apply a variety of algorithms to develop predictive models. It covers the essential aspects of predictive modeling — regression, decision trees, SVM, and neural networks — with emphasis on:

- **Evaluating** model performance using appropriate metrics and selecting the optimal model for a task.
- **Feature selection and engineering**, the bias–variance trade-off, overfitting and underfitting — managed via regularization and cross-validation.
- **Ensemble methods** — bagging, boosting, and stacking — to enhance predictive performance.
- Applying these techniques to **real-world predictive tasks** on unseen data.
- Building **proficiency in Python ML libraries** to implement and fine-tune models.

## What you'll learn

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
<span class="icon">📈</span>
### Supervised Learning
Linear & logistic regression, decision trees, random forests, and SVMs — model fitting, interpretation, and applications.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<span class="icon">🧩</span>
### Ensembles
Bagging, boosting, and stacking — gradient boosting machines, bootstrapping, aggregation, and feature importance.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<span class="icon">🧠</span>
### Neural Networks
Feedforward nets, backpropagation, CNNs, and Transformers — implemented hands-on with Keras 3.0.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<span class="icon">🎯</span>
### Model Evaluation
Precision, recall, F1, ROC; cross-validation; the bias–variance trade-off; and taming overfitting with regularization.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<span class="icon">🔍</span>
### Unsupervised Learning
Clustering (K-means, hierarchical, density-based) and dimensionality reduction (PCA, t-SNE) for segmentation & visualization.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<span class="icon">⚖️</span>
### ML in Practice
End-to-end projects across healthcare, finance, and retail — with a serious look at bias, fairness, and transparency.
:::
:::

## Syllabus

**1 · Review of ML Concepts** — overview & applications, conventions & metrics, basic statistics, data preparation and preprocessing.

**2 · Supervised Learning** — linear & logistic regression, decision trees & random forests (entropy, information gain), support vector machines (kernels, hyperplanes).

**3 · Unsupervised Learning** — clustering (K-means, hierarchical, density-based), dimensionality reduction (PCA, t-SNE), applications in segmentation & visualization.

**4 · Neural Networks & Deep Learning** — architecture, activations, feedforward & backpropagation; CNNs for vision; RNNs for sequences.

**5 · Ensemble Methods** — bagging, boosting, stacking; random forests; gradient boosting machines.

**6 · Model Evaluation** — accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC; cross-validation & bootstrapping; bias–variance trade-off; ridge & lasso.

**7 · Predictive Modelling in Practice** — applications in healthcare, finance, retail, manufacturing; ethics, fairness & transparency; an end-to-end project.

The date-wise breakdown with slides and notebooks lives on the [Schedule](schedule.qmd) page.

## Prerequisites

No hard prerequisites, but the following background helps you keep pace:

- **Python programming** — comfortable with NumPy, pandas, and matplotlib
- **Linear algebra** — vectors, matrices, and operations
- **Calculus** — derivatives and the chain rule
- **Probability & statistics** — distributions, expectations, hypothesis testing

### Preparation Materials

To refresh before the course begins:

- [Python Data Science Handbook](https://jakevdp.github.io/PythonDataScienceHandbook/) — Jake VanderPlas (Chapters 1–4)
- [Linear Algebra for Machine Learning](https://machinelearningmastery.com/linear-algebra-machine-learning/)
- [Khan Academy: Statistics & Probability](https://www.khanacademy.org/math/statistics-probability)
- [Mathematics for Machine Learning](https://mml-book.github.io/book/mml-book.pdf) — free PDF

## Reference Textbooks

**Primary references:**

1. **Mathematics for Machine Learning** — Deisenroth, Faisal & Ong · [Free PDF](https://mml-book.github.io/book/mml-book.pdf)
2. **An Introduction to Statistical Learning** — James, Witten, Hastie & Tibshirani · [Free online](https://www.statlearning.com/)
3. **Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning** — Christopher Bishop · [Free PDF](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/uploads/prod/2006/01/Bishop-Pattern-Recognition-and-Machine-Learning-2006.pdf)

**Additional references:**

4. **The Elements of Statistical Learning** — Friedman, Hastie & Tibshirani · [Free PDF](https://hastie.su.domains/ElemStatLearn/)
5. **Deep Learning** — Goodfellow, Bengio & Courville · [Free online](https://www.deeplearningbook.org/)
6. **Pattern Classification** — Duda, Hart & Stork
7. **Machine Learning** — Tom Mitchell
8. **Machine Learning: A Probabilistic Perspective** — Kevin Murphy

## Additional Learning Resources

**Online courses:**

- [NPTEL — Machine Learning](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=r4sgKrRL2Ys&list=PL1xHD4vteKYVpaIiy295pg6_SY5qznc77) — Balaraman Ravindran
- [CMU — Machine Learning](http://www.cs.cmu.edu/~ninamf/courses/601sp15/lectures.shtml) — Mitchell & Balcan
- [Coursera — Machine Learning](https://www.coursera.org/learn/machine-learning) — Andrew Ng
- [fast.ai — Machine Learning for Coders](https://www.fast.ai/)
- [fast.ai — Practical Deep Learning](https://course.fast.ai/)
- [UCI — Machine Learning](http://sli.ics.uci.edu/Classes/2015W-273a) — Alex Ihler
